content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 1990

Charles G. Abdelnour, J.D.
City Clerk, City of San Diego
City Administration Building
202 C Street
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-670

Dear Mr. Abdelnour:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTION

Is Together for Linda Bernhardt, a controlled committee
formed to support Linda Bernhardt's reelection to office, required
to file a campaign statement under Section 84200.5(f) if it
receives contributions or makes expenditures in opposition to an
effort to recall Ms. Bernhardt?

CONCLUSION

Only committees formed primarily to support or oppose the
qualification of a ballot measure, including a recall measure, are
required to file under Section 84200.5(f).

FACTS

The Together for Linda Bernhardt committee filed a statement
of organization on August 15, 1990, with the Secretary of State's
office. According to the statement of organization, the committee
was formed to support Linda Bernhardt's reelection to the city
council.

An effort was made to place the question of whether Ms.
Bernhardt should be recalled on the ballot. The Together for
Linda Bernhardt used its funds in opposition to the qualification
of the recall measure.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Charles G. Abdelnour, J.D.
Page Two

ANALYSIS

Section 84200.5(f) requires committees formed primarily to
support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure to file a
campaign statement no later than 21 days after any petitions are
filed in connection with the attempt to place the measure on the
ballot, or 21 days after the deadline for filing those petitions,
whichever is earlier.

A recall measure is included in the Act's definition of
"measure." Section 82043 defines a "measure" as:

...any constitutional amendment or other proposition which is
submitted to a popular vote at an election by action of a
legislature body, or which is submitted or is intended to be
submitted to a popular vote at an election by initiative,
referendum or recall procedure whether or not it qualifies
for the ballot.

Since the Together for Linda Bernhardt committee is not a
committee primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification
of a ballot measure, it is not required to file a campaign
statement under Section 84200.5(f). However, all payments the
committee makes, including those to oppose the qualification of
the recall measure, must be reported on the committee's regular
semi-annual or pre-election campaign statements. (Section 84211.)

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit an officeholder
from using campaign funds to oppose an effort to recall him or her
from office. (Hong Advice Letter, No. A-89-133, copy enclosed.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
